patient_profile.therapeutic_framing: Dual proteinopathy means MORE therapeutic targets, not fewer. Cross-seeding between alpha-synuclein and tau means therapies targeting either protein could benefit both pathologies.

action_items.title: Screen for AADvac1 PSP Platform Trial
action_items.description: The PSP Platform Trial at UCSF, UCSD, and Mass General is enrolling NOW (Q1 2026). AADvac1 targets tau protein — directly relevant to the PSP features. This is the most time-sensitive action item. Contact CurePSP or trial sites directly to inquire about eligibility screening.
action_items.contact: CurePSP (curepsp.org); Trial sites: UCSF Memory & Aging Center, UC San Diego, Massachusetts General Hospital

action_items.title: Discuss Prasinezumab Phase 3 Trial Enrollment
action_items.description: Roche's prasinezumab (anti-alpha-synuclein antibody) Phase 3 PADOVA trial is enrolling 900 patients at sites across the US. This targets the CONFIRMED alpha-synuclein pathology. Ask the neurologist about eligibility and nearby trial sites. ClinicalTrials.gov: NCT04777331.
action_items.contact: Roche/Genentech clinical trials; neurologist referral to trial site
action_items.deadline: Currently enrolling — discuss at next neurology visit

action_items.title: Request Tau PET Imaging + CSF Biomarker Panel
action_items.description: These diagnostic tests would clarify the extent of tau pathology and quantify alpha-synuclein seeding activity. Tau PET (flortaucipir) visualizes tau distribution. CSF alpha-synuclein seed amplification assay (SAA) quantifies seeding. CSF phospho-tau panel (p-tau 181, 217, 231) quantifies tau burden. These results inform trial eligibility and treatment strategy.
action_items.contact: Current neurologist; may need referral to academic movement disorder center

action_items.title: Discuss Lixisenatide (GLP-1 Agonist) with Neurologist
action_items.description: Lixisenatide showed reduced motor disability progression in NEJM 2024 Phase 2 trial (LixiPark). While not yet approved for PD, it's available as a diabetes medication and the neuroprotective evidence is strong (Tier 1). Some neurologists are prescribing off-label. Discuss risk/benefit with the care team.

action_items.title: Monitor ACI-7104.056 (VacSYn) Phase 2 Expansion
action_items.description: AC Immune's alpha-synuclein vaccine showed exceptional results: >20x antibody levels, 100% seroconversion, no UPDRS III progression at 74 weeks. Stage 2 expansion to 150 patients is expected. No action needed now, but this could become enrollable within 6-12 months. Keep monitoring.

action_items.title: Explore Dendritic Cell Vaccine Feasibility
action_items.description: Preclinical research shows dendritic cell vaccines can be dual-loaded with alpha-synuclein AND tau antigens — potentially addressing both pathologies simultaneously. This is still early-stage (Tier 3) but the dual proteinopathy makes this approach uniquely relevant. Ask the neurologist or an academic center about any open trials or compassionate use.

tracked_therapies.name: ACI-7104.056 (VacSYn)
tracked_therapies.mechanism: Peptide vaccine using SupraAntigen platform — mimics oligomerized alpha-synuclein conformation to generate antibodies that clear pathological aggregates
tracked_therapies.latest_update: Interim data: >20x anti-α-syn antibody levels vs baseline, 100% seroconversion rate, no UPDRS III motor score progression at 74 weeks. Stage 2 expansion (150 patients) expected.
tracked_therapies.relevance_to_patient: Directly targets confirmed α-syn pathology. Strong immunogenicity data suggests robust antibody response. If approved, could slow or halt synuclein-driven degeneration. Cross-seeding benefit possible (reducing α-syn may reduce tau templating).

tracked_therapies.name: UB-312
tracked_therapies.mechanism: Peptide vaccine targeting C-terminal region of alpha-synuclein using UBITh platform technology
tracked_therapies.latest_update: Phase 1b study in PD patients nearing completion. Safety and immunogenicity data expected in 2025.
tracked_therapies.relevance_to_patient: Another α-syn vaccine approach — different epitope than VacSYn. If VacSYn fails, UB-312 may succeed due to targeting different region of the protein.

tracked_therapies.name: PD01A / PD03A (AFFITOPE)
tracked_therapies.mechanism: Short peptide mimicking α-syn epitope (AFFITOPE technology) — generates antibodies without autoimmune risk of full-protein vaccines
tracked_therapies.latest_update: Demonstrated immunogenicity and acceptable safety profile in Phase 1. Further development status under review by AFFiRiS.
tracked_therapies.relevance_to_patient: Third α-syn vaccine approach. Lower autoimmune risk due to short peptide design. Earlier stage but diversifies the vaccine pipeline.

tracked_therapies.name: AADvac1
tracked_therapies.mechanism: Tau peptide vaccine targeting mis-disordered tau protein — selected for PSP Platform Trial based on Phase 1 safety and immunogenicity data
tracked_therapies.status: PSP Platform Trial Phase 2a — ENROLLING Q1 2026
tracked_therapies.latest_update: Selected as one of two regimens for the PSP Platform Trial. Enrolling at UCSF, UCSD, and Mass General. This is the first dedicated PSP immunotherapy trial with a tau vaccine.
tracked_therapies.relevance_to_patient: HIGHEST RELEVANCE — directly targets suspected tau pathology (PSP features). The PSP Platform Trial is specifically designed for patients with PSP-Richardson's phenotype. Enrollment is NOW.

tracked_therapies.name: AZP2006
tracked_therapies.mechanism: Restores lysosomal homeostasis and modulates progranulin — addresses tau clearance mechanism rather than directly binding tau
tracked_therapies.latest_update: Selected as the second regimen for the PSP Platform Trial alongside AADvac1. Novel mechanism targeting tau clearance rather than tau itself.
tracked_therapies.relevance_to_patient: Relevant for PSP features. Different mechanism than AADvac1 (clearance vs. immunotherapy) — if one fails, the other may work through a different pathway.

tracked_therapies.name: Anti-pT181 Mucosal Vaccine
tracked_therapies.mechanism: Nasal mucosal vaccine targeting phospho-tau181 epitope — non-invasive delivery, targets specific phosphorylation site implicated in tau seeding
tracked_therapies.latest_update: University of New Mexico developing mucosal (nasal) vaccine against phospho-tau181. Preclinical data encouraging. Enrollment expected early 2026.
tracked_therapies.relevance_to_patient: Targets tau phosphorylation specifically implicated in PSP. Non-invasive nasal delivery is attractive for patients with motor symptoms. Earlier stage but novel approach.

tracked_therapies.name: Prasinezumab
tracked_therapies.mechanism: Monoclonal antibody targeting aggregated alpha-synuclein — binds and promotes clearance of extracellular α-syn aggregates
tracked_therapies.latest_update: Roche's Phase 3 PADOVA trial is the largest α-syn immunotherapy trial in history. 900 patients across global sites. Phase 2b showed 28% slowing of motor progression in rapid progressors. Phase 3 expanded to broader PD population.
tracked_therapies.relevance_to_patient: Directly targets confirmed α-syn pathology. Most advanced α-syn antibody in clinical development. Phase 3 enrollment is ACTIVE — should discuss trial participation with neurologist.

tracked_therapies.name: Bepranemab
tracked_therapies.mechanism: Anti-tau monoclonal antibody targeting central region of tau protein — binds both 3R and 4R tau isoforms
tracked_therapies.status: Open-label extension in PSP — completing Nov 2026
tracked_therapies.latest_update: Open-label extension study in PSP patients ongoing, expected completion November 2026. Safety and preliminary efficacy data expected.
tracked_therapies.relevance_to_patient: Targets tau in PSP specifically. One of few monoclonal antibodies being tested in PSP (not just AD). Completion in Nov 2026 means results could inform treatment decisions.

tracked_therapies.name: Brain-Shuttled α-Syn Antibody
tracked_therapies.mechanism: Engineered antibody with transferrin receptor binding domain — crosses blood-brain barrier at 10-40x higher brain concentrations than standard antibodies
tracked_therapies.latest_update: Published in npj Parkinson's Disease 2025. Brain-shuttle technology achieves dramatically higher CNS penetration. Could solve the BBB problem that limits current antibody therapies.
tracked_therapies.relevance_to_patient: If current antibodies (prasinezumab) fail due to poor brain penetration, brain-shuttled versions could succeed. Technology platform could be applied to both α-syn and tau antibodies.

tracked_therapies.name: Lixisenatide
tracked_therapies.mechanism: GLP-1 receptor agonist — activates neuronal survival pathways, reduces neuroinflammation, promotes neurotrophic factor release. Originally a diabetes medication.
tracked_therapies.latest_update: NEJM 2024 (LixiPark trial): Lixisenatide reduced motor disability progression vs placebo over 12 months. First GLP-1 agonist to show disease-modifying signal in PD. Further Phase 3 trials in planning.
tracked_therapies.relevance_to_patient: Neuroprotective regardless of protein pathology — could benefit both α-syn and tau-driven degeneration. Already approved for diabetes (available off-label). Discuss with neurologist.

tracked_therapies.name: Exenatide
tracked_therapies.mechanism: GLP-1 receptor agonist — same class as lixisenatide but different pharmacokinetic profile
tracked_therapies.latest_update: Lancet 2025: Phase 3 trial of exenatide in PD was NEGATIVE — did not slow progression. However, different GLP-1RAs may differ in efficacy. Lixisenatide's positive result suggests the class isn't dead.
tracked_therapies.relevance_to_patient: Negative result for this specific GLP-1 agonist, but the class remains promising (lixisenatide was positive). Highlights that not all GLP-1RAs are equivalent for neurodegeneration.

tracked_therapies.name: BIIB101 (Anti-SNCA ASO)
tracked_therapies.mechanism: Antisense oligonucleotide targeting SNCA mRNA — reduces production of alpha-synuclein at the genetic level rather than clearing existing aggregates
tracked_therapies.latest_update: Ionis/Biogen antisense oligonucleotide reducing SNCA expression. Phase 1 study in MSA (another synucleinopathy). If successful, could expand to PD and dual proteinopathies.
tracked_therapies.relevance_to_patient: Upstream approach — stops α-syn production rather than clearing aggregates. Could be combined with immunotherapy (reduce production + clear existing). Currently in MSA but PD expansion possible.

tracked_therapies.name: Focused Ultrasound BBB Opening
tracked_therapies.mechanism: MRI-guided focused ultrasound transiently opens the blood-brain barrier, allowing antibodies and other therapeutics to reach brain tissue at much higher concentrations
tracked_therapies.status: Phase 1 safe in PD dementia — combination trials next
tracked_therapies.latest_update: Phase 1 demonstrated safety of focused ultrasound BBB opening in PD dementia patients. The next frontier is combining FUS with immunotherapy — open the BBB, then deliver antibodies directly to affected brain regions.
tracked_therapies.relevance_to_patient: Could dramatically improve efficacy of any antibody therapy (prasinezumab, bepranemab, brain-shuttled Abs). Particularly relevant for dual proteinopathy — could deliver both α-syn and tau antibodies in same session.

research_updates.title: Initial Research Compilation — Dual Proteinopathy Treatment Landscape
research_updates.summary: Comprehensive analysis of the treatment landscape for dual alpha-synuclein + tau proteinopathy. Identified 13 tracked therapies across 6 categories: 3 alpha-synuclein vaccines (VacSYn, UB-312, AFFITOPE), 3 tau-targeting approaches (AADvac1, AZP2006, anti-pT181), 3 monoclonal antibodies (prasinezumab, bepranemab, brain-shuttled Ab), 2 GLP-1 neuroprotectants (lixisenatide, exenatide), 1 gene therapy (BIIB101), and 1 delivery technology (focused ultrasound). The key insight: cross-seeding between α-syn and tau means therapies targeting either protein could benefit both pathologies. The dual proteinopathy is an OPPORTUNITY — more therapeutic targets, not fewer.
research_updates.implications_for_patient: The treatment landscape for dual proteinopathy is the richest it has ever been. Two trial enrollment windows are open NOW (AADvac1 for tau, prasinezumab for α-syn). GLP-1 neuroprotection (lixisenatide) is available today. The next 12-24 months will see VacSYn expansion, bepranemab results, and focused ultrasound combinations. The strategic priority is: (1) enroll in available trials, (2) pursue biomarker clarification, (3) discuss neuroprotection with the care team.

diagnostic_recommendations.purpose: Visualize tau protein distribution in the brain. Can confirm PSP pattern (subcortical tau in basal ganglia, brainstem, cerebellum) vs Alzheimer's pattern (cortical tau). Critical for trial eligibility and treatment planning.
diagnostic_recommendations.availability: Major academic medical centers with PET imaging capability. May require referral from neurologist.

diagnostic_recommendations.purpose: Quantify alpha-synuclein seeding activity in cerebrospinal fluid. Complements the positive skin biopsy by measuring how actively α-syn is misfolding and spreading. Higher seeding activity may indicate faster progression or better response to anti-α-syn therapy.
diagnostic_recommendations.availability: Research labs and some academic centers. Lumbar puncture required.

diagnostic_recommendations.purpose: Quantify tau phosphorylation burden. Different phospho-tau species reflect different aspects of tau pathology. p-tau217 is the most specific for tau aggregation. Results help quantify the 'tau side' of the dual proteinopathy.
diagnostic_recommendations.availability: Clinical labs (Quest, LabCorp offer some; research panels at academic centers more comprehensive). Lumbar puncture required.

diagnostic_recommendations.purpose: Screen for genetic variants that influence disease course and unlock gene-targeted therapies. GBA mutations (most common PD risk gene) have specific therapeutics in trials. LRRK2 mutations have kinase inhibitors in development. MAPT mutations affect tau biology. SNCA variants affect α-syn expression.
diagnostic_recommendations.availability: Genetic counseling + commercial genetic testing. Some covered by insurance with PD diagnosis.

diagnostic_recommendations.purpose: Quantify dopamine transporter density in the striatum. Confirms and quantifies dopaminergic neurodegeneration. May be required for eligibility in some clinical trials (prasinezumab Phase 3 requires DaT-SPECT confirmation).
diagnostic_recommendations.availability: Nuclear medicine departments at most hospitals. Outpatient procedure.
